text.title: Support SLA — Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the shape of `SLAPolicy` and `CannedReply` outputs.

list.item: `tiers` — array of 3 (Community, Paid, Enterprise) with `first_reply_sla_hours`, `business_hours_only: bool`, `channels`
`escalation_triggers` — array (≥ 4) of concrete events per r4
`escalation_check` — boolean — does this scenario need escalation evaluation?

antipattern.description: SLA hours expressed without `business_hours_only: true` for Community / Paid tiers.
antipattern.reason: Violates r2. Wall-clock SLA destroys weekend.
Violates r1. Sub-tiers collapse into Paid.

antipattern.description: `escalation_triggers` contains generic ("urgent ticket") not concrete events.
antipattern.reason: Violates r4. Generic triggers reactivate the squeaky-wheel problem.
Replies without specifics feel robotic and erode trust. Customer must see the operator looked at THEIR ticket.
Solo brand depends on warmth; bad tone churns paying users.

list.item: Add a 4th tier ONLY for enterprise sub-contracts with named customer; never publish publicly.
Tighten Paid SLA to 12h business hours for products with mission-critical workloads.
Translate canned replies into customer-language for non-English audiences.
Each tier shows business-hours flag and timezone.
Escalation triggers concrete (data loss, security, churn-within-7d, 3+ same break).
Out-of-hours auto-reply references the SLA page.
Canned reply set covers: bug, billing, feature-request, complaint.
